summary: Tested MR Order Form role-based rates feature. All 7 backend tests passed. All frontend UI tests passed. The feature correctly displays role-based rates (rate_doctors, rate_medicals, rate_agencies) based on customer entity_type.
seed_data_creation: Used existing test data: MR account (Test MR), customer (Dr. Test Address with entity_type=doctor), item (Amoxicillin 500mg with rate_doctors=65)
context_for_next_testing_agent: MR Order Form role-based rates feature fully tested and working. Key components verified: 1) GET /api/mr/customers returns customers with entity_type field (doctor/medical/agency). 2) GET /api/mr/items returns items with rate_doctors, rate_medicals, rate_agencies fields. 3) MROrders.jsx getRoleRate() function (lines 101-107) correctly selects rate based on customer entity_type. 4) Item search dropdown shows 'Rate: ₹{roleRate}' in blue (line 372). 5) Selected item card shows 'Default Rate: ₹{defaultRate}' in blue (line 411). 6) Rate input is pre-filled with role rate but editable (line 424-428). 7) Offers display per role in both search dropdown and item cards.

MR Login.POST /api/mr/login: PASS - Returns access_token and mr object

Customers API.GET /api/mr/customers returns entity_typ: PASS - All customers have entity_type field (doctor/medical/agency)

Items API.GET /api/mr/items returns role rates: PASS - Items have rate_doctors, rate_medicals, rate_agencies fields
Items API.GET /api/mr/items?search=Amox: PASS - Amoxicillin 500mg has rate_doctors=65, rate_medicals=35, rate_agencies=90

Frontend UI.Offers display per role: PASS - '15% off' and 'Buy 20 pcs at Rs.80/- only' shown
